title: Susan Sarandon Says She Was Banned From Hollywood After Calling for Gaza Ceasefire: I Feel ‘Repression and Censorship’ in United States
content:
ByWilliam Earl

Susan Sarandonis speaking out about her current film and television career, saying that after she spoke out and called for a ceasefire in Gaza that she was unable to take on roles in the United States.

“I was fired by my agency, specifically for marching and speaking out about Gaza, for asking for a ceasefire,” Sarandon said. “It became impossible for me to even be on television. I don’t know lately if it’s changed. I couldn’t do any major film or anything connected with Hollywood. I found agents ultimately in England and in Italy, and I work there. I just did a film in Italy, and I did a play at the Old Vic for a number of months. I know this Italian director that just hired me — he was toldnotto hire me, so that’s still recently. He didn’t listen, but they had that conversation. Right now, I kind of specialize in tiny films with directors who have never directed, in independent films.”

The “Thelma and Louise” star also said that Spain has more intellectual freedom than the United States.

“In a place where you feel repression and censorship, to see Spain and to see the president and what he says and the support that he’s giving about Gaza, and to have actors like Javier Bardem come forward with such a strong voice, is so important to us in the United States,” she said.

Her comments came during a Feb. 27 discussion ahead of receiving the International Goya award in recognition of her career, including “Dead Man Walking” and “Atlantic City.” The Spanish Film Academy’s Goya Awards ceremony is set to take place on Feb. 28 in Barcelona.

In 2023, Sarandon wasdropped by UTAafter she spoke at a pro-Palestine rally in New York City.

Watch the full discussion below, courtesy of Reuters.
title_zh: 蘇珊·莎蘭登(Susan Sarandon)表示，她在呼籲加沙停火後被好萊塢禁入：我感受到美國的「壓迫和審查」。
content_zh:
威廉·厄爾(William Earl)報導

蘇珊·薩蘭登(Susan Sarandon)談到了她目前的電影和電視事業，表示在她呼籲加沙停火後，她無法在美國接演角色。

薩蘭登說：「我被我的經紀公司解僱，特別是因為我在加沙遊行並發聲，要求停火。對我來說，甚至上電視都變得不可能。我不知道最近是否有所改變。我無法參加任何主要電影或與好萊塢有關的事情。最終我在英國和意大利找到了經紀人，我在那裡工作。我剛在意大利拍了一部電影，並在老維克劇院(Old Vic)演出了一段時間。我認識一位剛雇用我的意大利導演——他被告知不要雇用我，所以這仍然是最近的事。他沒有聽從，但他們有過那樣的對話。現在，我有點專注於與從未導過演的導演合作的小型獨立電影。」

這位《西部女郎(Thelma and Louise)》的明星還表示，西班牙比美國擁有更多的知識自由。

她說：「在一個感受到壓迫和審查的地方，看到西班牙，看到總統及其對加沙的支持，以及哈維爾·巴登(Javier Bardem)等演員以如此強烈的聲音站出來，對我們在美國來說是非常重要的。」

她的評論是在2月27日的一次討論中發表的，該討論是為了表彰她的職業生涯，包括《死者漫步(Dead Man Walking)》和《大西洋城(Atlantic City)》。西班牙電影學院的戈雅獎(Goya Awards)頒獎典禮定於2月28日在巴塞隆納舉行。

在2023年，薩蘭登因在紐約市的一場支持巴勒斯坦的集會上發言而被UTA解僱。

以下是完整的討論視頻，感謝路透社(Reuters)提供。

薩蘭登說：「我被我的經紀公司解僱，特別是因為我在加沙遊行並發聲，要求停火。對我來說，甚至上電視都變得不可能。我不知道最近是否有所改變。我無法參加任何主要電影或與好萊塢有關的事情。最終我在英國和意大利找到了經紀人，我在那裡工作。
